title: OSMM Creative Strategy Object
description: The creative direction for an offering or campaign: the central creative platform (big idea), the creative themes/territories, the emotional strategy, the creative system & experience concepts (the former Experience Design object, absorbed here), and channel-specific creative requirements. Canonical schema for object_type 'creative_strategy'. The osmm-creative-strategy-builder skill references this file; it is the single source of truth for the object's shape. Creative Strategy is HOW we express the message — the message itself cascades from Brand Context (brand promise/pillars) and Product Context product_messaging (product value pillars) and resolves per persona/stage in the Journey. Distinct from the Content Strategy (the content plan) and Brand Context (the durable brand voice/identity it operates within). It resolves sub-processes 5.2, 5.5, 5.6, and 5.8.

osmm_version.description: OSMM schema version this instance conforms to (semantic version).

creative_strategy_id.description: Stable, human-readable id. Prefix 'CRS-' + lowercase hyphen slug. Never changes once assigned.

version.description: Instance version (e.g. '1.0'). Bump on revision; the id stays stable.

name.description: A readable label for the creative strategy, usually entity + offering or campaign (e.g. 'IBM watsonx Creative Strategy').

creative_platform.description: The central creative idea — the big idea / creative platform that organizes all expression. The one creative concept everything ladders up to.

creative_themes.description: The creative themes / territories the platform explores — the recurring creative angles or motifs (5.2).

emotional_strategy.description: The emotional approach — the feeling the creative is meant to evoke and how it gets there (5.2). Optional.

experience_concepts.description: The creative system & experience concepts — the former Experience Design object, absorbed here (5.5). Each entry is a named concept and what it is.

concept.description: The experience concept's name — a creative-system element or experience idea (e.g. a signature interaction, a visual system, an experiential motif).

description.description: What the concept is and how it expresses the platform.

channel_creative.description: Channel-specific creative requirements — how the platform translates per channel (5.6). Each entry pairs a channel with its requirements.

channel.description: The channel or medium (e.g. social, OOH, web, email, video, in-store).

requirements.description: The creative requirements / treatment for this channel — what the creative must do or look like here.

creative_principles.description: Creative guardrails / do's — the principles that keep execution on platform. Optional.

linked_brand_context.description: Id of the Brand Context whose voice/identity this operates within. 'BRC-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_product.description: Id of the Product Context this creative is for. 'PRD-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_personas.description: Ids of the Persona objects this creative speaks to. 'PER-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_campaign_strategy.description: Id of the Campaign Strategy if this creative strategy is scoped to a specific campaign. 'CMS-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_business_context.description: Id of the owning Business Context. 'BIZ-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

source.description: One line: what source(s) this was built from and approximate date. Optional.
